LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DARCY WALLACE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. SERVICE ON DEFENDANT WAS NOT MADE WITHIN TWO YEARS OF THE COMPLAINT BEING FILED - CCP SECTION 583.410 AND 583.420(A)(1). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE WHY THE CASE SHOULD NOT BE DISMISSED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT BEEN DILIGENT IN PROSECUTING THIS COMPLAINT. THE FACTS UNDERLYING THIS ACTION ARE MORE THAN SEVEN YEARS OLD, AND THE PROBATE ESTATE WAS CLOSED IN MARIN ALMOST SIX YEARS AGO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(302/CWW)JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Continued To Apr-12-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DARCY WALLACE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Mark W. Epstein, Esq. of Seiler Epstein Ziegler & Applegate LLP (415) 979-0500 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Service on defendant was not made within two years of the complaint being filed - CCP section 583.410 and 583.420(a)(1). Plaintiff fails to show good cause why the case should not be dismissed. Plaintiff has not been diligent in prosecuting this complaint. The facts underlying this action are more than seven years old, and the probate estate was closed in Marin almost six years ago. Order signed in open Court. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
